skill.name: design-to-production
skill.description: Guided workflow for converting HTML design prototypes into production React components with glassmorphism styling, shadcn/ui mapping, scaffolding, and quality validation.
skill.summary: Convert HTML prototypes into production React components with guided mapping, templates, and validation.
skill.author: ArieGoldkin

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but most reviewed markdown and template hits are false positives caused by code fences, inline backticks, file paths, and styling examples. The executable shell scripts are legitimate workflow helpers, but they accept user-controlled paths and names with limited normalization, so publication should include a warning and hardening guidance.

medium_findings.title: Path Traversal Risk in HTML Structure Extraction
medium_findings.description: The extraction script joins a user-supplied HTML path to PROJECT_ROOT without rejecting traversal segments or enforcing the expected prototype directory. It then reads that path with grep and writes derived data to a skill output file. This appears intended for local design analysis, but a crafted path could make the script inspect files outside the project when a user runs it.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is directly present in an executable shell script and uses user-controlled path input. Confidence is below very high because there is no network exfiltration and the script is designed for local, user-invoked analysis.

medium_findings.title: User-Controlled Output Path in Component Scaffolding
medium_findings.description: The scaffolding script builds MODULE_PATH and OUTPUT_FILE from user-controlled module and component names. It checks whether the module directory exists, but it does not normalize the final path or restrict component names to safe identifiers before writing the generated TSX file.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script clearly writes to a path derived from user input. The risk is moderate because normal usage targets known frontend modules and existing files require an overwrite confirmation.

medium_findings.title: Validation Script Can Inspect Arbitrary Local Files
medium_findings.description: The validation script accepts a component path and falls back to using that path directly if it exists outside the expected frontend source tree. It reads line counts and matching snippets from the selected file, which could expose limited local file content in terminal output.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The arbitrary-path fallback is visible and the script reads the target file. The impact is limited because output is local, pattern-based, and no network or secret collection behavior was found.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Ruby Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged many markdown code fences, inline file paths, and TSX examples as Ruby or shell backtick execution. Reviewed examples are documentation snippets for running local scripts or showing React markup, not executable Ruby code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Multiple reviewed locations are markdown examples or code fences, and no Ruby source files were present in the scanned file list. Confidence is high that these specific static findings are false positives.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Styling and Text False Positives
low_findings.description: The high-severity weak cryptography alerts did not correspond to cryptographic code in the reviewed files. The sampled locations contain titles, documentation, CSS class names, or React component examples rather than hashing, encryption, or password handling logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Reviewed locations do not contain cryptographic APIs or security-sensitive algorithms. Confidence is high for dismissal, though not every static line was individually inspected due to repeated pattern noise.

dangerous_patterns.title: Relative Path Concatenation Without Canonical Boundary Check
dangerous_patterns.description: Shell scripts concatenate user input with project paths and test or write the resulting paths without canonicalizing them against an allowed directory. This pattern can allow unexpected reads or writes when traversal segments are supplied.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The code uses quoted variables, which reduces shell injection risk, but it lacks a real path boundary check. The finding is grounded in executable script behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Template Substitution Uses Unvalidated Identifiers
dangerous_patterns.description: The scaffolder injects component and module names into sed replacement expressions and generated source paths without validating identifier format. Unexpected characters can break generation or write files in unintended locations.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The issue is visible in executable script logic, but it is more reliability and path-control risk than confirmed malicious behavior.

content.user_title: Convert HTML Designs to React Components
content.value_statement: Turning static HTML prototypes into production React code can be slow and inconsistent. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through analysis, component mapping, scaffolding, and validation.
actual_capabilities: Extracts component names, glassmorphism classes, interactive element counts, and layout patterns from HTML prototypes.
Guides mapping from HTML elements to shadcn/ui components and approved glassmorphism classes.
Scaffolds React TSX components from interactive, display, or layout templates.
Creates components under frontend module directories using consistent import and interface conventions.
Validates generated components for line count, interface naming, any types, glass classes, and import patterns.
Provides reference guides and examples for common glassmorphism component patterns.
limitations: It assumes the target project uses the documented frontend module structure and shared UI imports.
It does not implement business logic automatically beyond template TODO markers.
The shell scripts need careful path input because they do not fully normalize user-provided paths.
Validation is pattern-based and does not replace TypeScript, lint, accessibility, or visual regression testing.

use_cases.title: Prototype Conversion for Frontend Engineers
use_cases.description: Convert a completed HTML prototype into a typed React component while preserving layout, styling classes, and interaction points.

use_cases.title: Design System Adoption for Product Teams
use_cases.description: Map prototype elements to shadcn/ui components and approved glassmorphism classes before implementation begins.

use_cases.title: Consistency Checks for Technical Leads
use_cases.description: Validate generated components against local rules for size, naming, import patterns, and unsafe TypeScript usage.

prompt_templates.title: Convert a Simple Prototype
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner conversion from one HTML file
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this HTML prototype into a production React component: [path]. Use the design-to-production workflow and show the mapping decisions first.

prompt_templates.title: Map Components Before Coding
prompt_templates.scenario: Review design system choices before scaffolding
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze [path] and propose the component name, module, shadcn/ui mappings, glassmorphism classes, and template type before generating files.

prompt_templates.title: Scaffold and Validate a Component
prompt_templates.scenario: Create a component in an existing frontend module
prompt_templates.prompt: Scaffold [ComponentName] in the [module] module using the best template for [path]. Then validate the generated component and summarize issues.

prompt_templates.title: Productionize a Complex Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert [path] into production React for the [module] module. Preserve approved glassmorphism classes, avoid any types, keep files under 180 lines, and list follow-up tests.

output_examples.input: A user provides a glassmorphism hints panel prototype and asks for a React implementation.
output_examples.output: The skill identifies an interactive card template, maps buttons to shared UI Button components, creates a HintsPanel scaffold, and reports validation results.

output_examples.input: A user asks whether a stats section should be a display card or layout section.
output_examples.output: The skill compares the structure, recommends the display-card template for repeated metrics, and lists the required props and class mappings.

output_examples.input: A user validates an existing component after filling in TODOs.
output: Flags interface names without the I prefix.
Warns about any types, invalid glass classes, or excessive relative imports.

best_practices: Run extraction only on known prototype files inside the expected design iteration directory.
Confirm component name, module placement, template type, and UI mappings before scaffolding files.
Run the validator plus the project test, lint, and typecheck commands before merging generated components.
anti_patterns: Do not pass arbitrary filesystem paths to the scripts without reviewing what they will read or write.
Do not treat generated TODO scaffolds as complete production logic.
Do not bypass accessibility, responsive layout, or visual review because the pattern validator passes.

faq.question: What kind of prototypes does this skill support?
faq.answer: It is designed for HTML prototypes that use glassmorphism classes and can be mapped to React, TypeScript, and shadcn/ui components.

faq.question: Does it generate a complete production component?
faq.answer: It generates a structured TSX scaffold and validation guidance. Developers still complete business logic and final review.

faq.question: Can it choose the right template automatically?
faq.answer: It suggests a template from detected elements, such as interactive-card for prototypes with buttons and display-card for read-only content.

faq.question: Does it work outside the documented project structure?
faq.answer: Not reliably. The scripts assume a frontend source tree with modules, shared UI imports, and local glassmorphism styles.

faq.question: Why is the security rating medium?
faq.answer: The skill includes useful shell scripts, but their path handling is not fully normalized. Use trusted paths and review generated files.

faq.question: What should I run after using this skill?
faq.answer: Run the included validator, then run the project TypeScript, lint, test, and visual review process.
